steps.description: Grind 1 g Zn powders (purity 99.99%) and place at one end of a ceramic boat. Place silicon substrate at the other end of the same ceramic boat.
Etch silicon substrate using hydrofluoric acid for about 10 min, then ultrasonically clean successively in acetone, ethanol, and deionized water for 15 min each step.
Deposit a 10 nm Au film on the surface of the cleaned silicon substrate by sputtering.
Place the ceramic boat with Zn powder and Au-coated silicon substrate into a quartz vial inside a horizontal-tube furnace. Place another boat with 4 mL water at the gas inlet port of the furnace.

Vacuum the system to kPa level using a mechanical pump, then heat the furnace to 730°C at a rate of 20°C/min and keep for 40 min under Ar flow of 50 sccm. Ar flow carries water vapor into the quartz vial to provide oxygen for oxidation of Zn. ZnO nanostructures grow via vapor-liquid-solid (VLS) mechanism using Au nanoparticles as catalysts.
Allow the furnace to cool down. A bluish-white layer is observed on the substrate surface.

synthesis.notes: Various ZnO nanostructures (nanowires, nanorods, nanoneedles, nanocombs) were obtained simultaneously on the same silicon substrate in one experimental run. The variation in nanostructure morphology is attributed to spatial differences in Zn and O2 concentration distribution inside the quartz vial. Au nanoparticles serve as catalysts in the VLS growth mechanism. Native defects such as zinc interstitials and oxygen vacancies may lead to n-type semiconducting behavior of ZnO.

steps.description: Substrate preparation: Etch silicon substrate using hydrofluoric acid for about 10 min, then ultrasonically clean successively in acetone, ethanol, and deionized water for 15 min for each step.
Deposit a 10 nm Au film on the surface of the silicon substrate by sputtering.
Grind 1 g Zn powders and place at the end of a ceramic boat. Place the silicon substrate at the other end of the same boat.
Place the boat in a quartz vial inside a horizontal-tube furnace. Place another boat with 4 mL water at the gas direction of the port of the horizontal-tube furnace.
Vacuum the system by a mechanical pump to KPa level.
Heat the furnace to reaction temperature 730°C at a rate of 20°C/min, and keep for 40 min under argon (Ar) flow of 50 sccm.
Allow the furnace to cool down.

steps.description: Silicon substrate etched with hydrofluoric acid for 10 min, then ultrasonically cleaned in acetone, ethanol, and deionized water for 15 min each.
Deposit a 10 nm Au film on the silicon substrate surface by sputtering.

Place 1 g Zn powder at one end of a ceramic boat and the prepared silicon substrate at the other end. Place the boat in a quartz vial inside a horizontal-tube furnace. Place another boat with 4 mL water at the gas direction of the furnace port.

Vacuum the system to KPa level, then heat the furnace to 730°C at 20°C/min under Ar flow of 50 sccm, and keep at 730°C for 40 min. Water vapor provides oxygen for Zn oxidation. Zn vaporizes and diffuses to the substrate, forming ZnO nanostructures via VLS growth.
Cool down the furnace naturally.
